items.title: Senolytics Reduce Markers of Aging in Human Trial
items.summary: A small clinical trial reports that a dasatinib plus quercetin regimen reduced circulating SASP biomarkers in adults over 65. Participants showed improved grip strength and reduced inflammatory markers over 12 weeks.

items.title: High-Protein Breakfast Linked to Better Metabolic Markers
items.summary: Researchers found that participants who consumed at least 30g of protein at breakfast had significantly better postprandial glucose control and reported higher satiety through midday compared to carbohydrate-heavy breakfasts.

items.title: Sleep Fragmentation Accelerates Cognitive Decline in Over-50s
items.summary: A longitudinal NIA-funded study following 4,200 adults over 8 years found that frequent nighttime awakenings were independently associated with faster cognitive decline, even after controlling for total sleep duration.

items.title: Exercise Mimetics: Where the Field Stands in 2026
items.summary: An overview of current exercise mimetic compounds under investigation, including AICAR analogs and newer mitochondrial uncouplers. Most remain in preclinical stages, with no clear candidate yet matching the broad benefits of actual exercise.

items.title: Fiber Intake and Gut Microbiome Diversity: New Dose-Response Data
items.summary: A controlled feeding study shows that increasing fiber intake from 15g to 35g per day over 6 weeks significantly increased gut microbial diversity and short-chain fatty acid production, with the largest gains seen between 25g and 35g.

items.title: Walking Speed as a Biomarker of Aging: Updated Guidelines
items.summary: NIA releases updated reference ranges for gait speed as a functional aging biomarker. Adults maintaining over 1.0 m/s walking speed showed consistently better outcomes across multiple aging domains in a 15,000-participant meta-analysis.
